title: Filter function creates a cartesian product when used with a view
description:
Join reordering is generating a cross product instead of pushing filter function down.
The optimizer is not handling filter functions that require 3 or more relations.
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t1 (a1 INT NOT NULL, a2 INT, p DOUBLE CHECK (p >= 0));
CREATE TABLE t2 (a1 INT PRIMARY KEY, p DOUBLE DEFAULT 0.0);
CREATE TABLE t3 (a1 INT, a2 INT NOT NULL, p DOUBLE);
CREATE VIEW t4 AS SELECT t3.a1 AS a1, t3.a2 AS a2, t2.p AS a3 FROM t3 JOIN t2 ON t3.a2 = t2.a1;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT t1.a1, t1.a2, t4.a1, t4.a2, t4.a3 FROM t1 JOIN t4 ON t1.a1 = t4.a1) AS foo;
key_bug_features: Filter function with three or more relations causes cross product
